title: Noun にとどまらず～も
titleEn: This expresses the idea of 'not only...but also', 'not stopping at...'.
structure: Noun + にとどまらず + Noun + も
structureEn: Noun + にとどまらず + Noun + も
explanation: This expresses the idea of 'not only...but also', 'not stopping at...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This grammar point is used to express that something is not limited to just one thing (the thing before にとどまらず), but also includes other things (the thing after も). It can be translated as 'not only... but also...' or 'not stopping at...'.

title: Noun にひきかえ Noun は
structure: Noun 1 + にひきかえ + Noun 2 + は
structureEn: Noun 1 + にひきかえ + Noun 2 + は
explanation: Represents 'in contrast to', 'as opposed to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun にひきかえ Noun は is used in Japanese to express contrast or opposition between two nouns. It translates to 'as opposed to' or 'in contrast to' in English. The first noun represents what is being contrasted against and the second noun represents the main subject.

title: Noun にもまして
explanation: This expresses 'Even more than; exceeding'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The Nounにもまして grammar point is used to express that something is even more or exceeds the noun that it’s being compared to. It translates to expressions like 'even more than' or 'exceeding' in English. It is used when making a comparison to emphasize the greater degree of something.

title: Noun によらず
explanation: Regardless of, irrespective of. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～によらず grammar point is used to express the meaning 'regardless of' or 'irrespective of' in English. It is used when the outcome does not change regardless of differences in conditions.

title: Noun に先駆けて
explanation: Meaning 'ahead of', 'before', or 'preceding in time'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point 〜に先駆けて indicates something happening ahead or before something else in terms of time. Typically used in written language or formal speech.

title: Noun に即した Noun
titleEn: Expresses the idea of 'in accordance with', 'based on'.
explanation: Expresses the idea of 'in accordance with', 'based on'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun に即した Noun' is used to express the idea of something being done in accordance with or based on something else. Usually, the first noun is principle, rule, reality, etc. and the second noun could be action, method, plan, etc.

title: Noun に即して Verb
titleEn: The grammar point Noun に即して Verb is used to mean 'to act in accordance with', 'in line with' or 'based on'.
explanation: The grammar point Noun に即して Verb is used to mean 'to act in accordance with', 'in line with' or 'based on'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The formation Noun に即して Verb is used when someone is doing something based on or in accordance with some norms, rules, decisions or principles. This structure is often used in business or official contexts.

title: Noun に言わせれば
titleEn: Expresses 'according to', 'if you ask', 'in the opinion of'.
explanation: Expresses 'according to', 'if you ask', 'in the opinion of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The に言わせれば grammar point is used when we want to express someone's opinion or viewpoint. It can be translated as 'according to', 'if you ask', or 'in the opinion of'. This form is mostly used in formal or written language.

title: Noun に限ったことではない
titleEn: Indicates that something is not only limited to; 'not only', 'not just'.
structure: Noun + に限ったことではない
structureEn: Noun + に限ったことではない
explanation: Indicates that something is not only limited to; 'not only', 'not just'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This grammar point is used to express that the statement is not only limited to the noun mentioned before it. In English, it can be translated as 'not only', 'not just', or sometimes 'not necessarily'. It underscores that what is being said is also true for other things or situations, not just for the specific noun stated.

title: Noun に限ったことでもない
structure: Noun + に限ったことでもない
structureEn: Noun + に限ったことでもない
explanation: 'Not limited to'; 'not just', 'not exclusively'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point 'Noun に限ったことでもない' typically expresses that something is not only limited to a specific instance or category. It insinuates that there are also other instances or categories involved. In English, it can be translated as 'not limited to', 'not just', or 'not exclusively'.
